context: Vague request analysis: agent must decompose 'make search better' into concrete gaps using the existing spec as a baseline, not jump to solutions.

checklist.name: Output file created
checklist.description: A file named search-improvement-prep.md is produced

checklist.name: Existing coverage summarized
checklist.description: Document summarizes what the current search spec already covers (query interface, TF-IDF ranking, filters, pagination, indexing)

checklist.name: Does not propose solutions
checklist.description: Document focuses on identifying gaps and questions — does NOT jump to proposing implementation changes (e.g. does NOT say 'switch to Elasticsearch' or 'add caching')

checklist.name: Ambiguity of 'slow' analyzed
checklist.description: Document identifies that 'slow' is ambiguous — could mean query latency, indexing speed, perceived UI slowness, etc.

checklist.name: Ambiguity of 'doesn't find' analyzed
checklist.description: Document identifies that 'doesn't find what they need' is ambiguous — could mean relevance ranking, missing fields, typo tolerance, partial matching, etc.

checklist.name: Questions are specific and bounded
checklist.description: Interview questions are specific (e.g. 'What is the current p95 query latency?' not 'What's wrong with search?')

checklist.name: Questions are individual items
checklist.description: Questions are listed as separate numbered items, not bundled or multi-part

checklist.name: No redundant questions
checklist.description: Questions do not ask about things already documented in the spec (e.g. does NOT ask 'what fields are searched?' — that's already in the spec)

checklist.name: Performance gap identified
checklist.description: Gap list notes that the existing spec has no performance requirements (no SLAs, no latency targets, no index size limits)

checklist.name: No implementation attempted
checklist.description: Agent does NOT modify any source files or create a new spec — this is a preparation task only
